commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0cd0d18975ac109881df99c0079cd586ee88e1c0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds broad bounded-read and fail-closed hardening for malformed plugin, schema, provider, Codex, Gateway/MCP, and Plugin SDK metadata plus focused fuzz-style tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced app metadata regression: current main preserves app/list records directly, while the diff sets three readable AppInfo metadata fields to null. For the broader hostile-metadata bug class, the source paths and PR tests support the reproduction, but I did not run a live third-party plugin repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve app inventory metadata; maintainers still need to decide the broader compatibility risks after that repair.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened malformed plugin/tool/schema metadata boundaries so hostile or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, diagnosed, or treated as absent instead of crashing provider routing, runtime tool resolution, Gateway/MCP catalogs, Codex prompt/report paths, plugin SDK helpers, bundle MCP projection/materialization, Codex dynamic-tool bridge setup, plugin-tools MCP execution, and Plugin SDK host-hook/HTTP/provider-like registration.
- Kept fixtures synthetic: tests and notes use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, `mockplugin_status`, `mockplugin_store`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest update guards provider-like Plugin SDK registrations: unreadable provider ids now produce registry diagnostics and skip only the malformed provider, healthy sibling providers keep loading, and class-backed provider instances are preserved for runtime methods.
- Recent update also guards Plugin SDK HTTP route registration: unreadable route fields now diagnose and skip only the malformed route, route handler/auth/match fields are validated, and `nodeCapability` is copied from bounded reads instead of raw spread.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action/bundle-MCP/Codex dynamic-tool/Plugin-SDK metadata can crash, widen decisions, or bypass schema quarantine before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, Gateway catalog rendering, MCP loopback handling, approval forwarding, manifest/capability auth gating, provider payload conversion, prompt-cache setup, Codex reporting/instruction generation, plugin SDK normalization, plugin host-hook registration, HTTP route registration, provider-like registration, agent-core tool lookup, cached plugin descriptor execution, static plugin tool registration, bundled capability runtime capture, bundle MCP catalog projection, bundle MCP catalog materialization, Codex dynamic-tool bridge setup, plugin-tools MCP execution, lifecycle/event host-hook registration, session scheduler/action host-hook registration, session extension/trusted policy host-hook registration, reload/node host/node invoke/security audit registration, service/gateway discovery service registration, or command registration completes.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`, local focused lint/format checks, local autoreview, synthetic/private-name scrub, and AWS Crabbox Linux `pnpm check:changed`. Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable in this shell because `blacksmith auth login` is required.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Pushed branch head `0cd0d18975ac109881df99c0079cd586ee88e1c0`. The current stack was last rebased onto `origin/main` `4b9a80d8958ad6d0e880e638a9d7f83ebb898f24`; `origin/main` advanced to `beb665212c90d9bc4d911b2cac63d4c56e1c692a` after focused proof, so I did not chase another rebase.
- Latest provider-like focused proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/registry.provider-like.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Latest provider-like focused format/diff checks: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/registry.ts src/plugins/registry.provider-like.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.
- Latest provider-like focused lint: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/registry.ts src/plugins/registry.provider-like.test.ts`.
- Local review for the provider-like registration slice: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`, rerun clean after fixing the accepted provider-instance compatibility finding.
- Recent HTTP route focused proof after rebase: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/http-registry.test.ts src/config/sessions.cache.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Recent HTTP route focused lint/format after rebase: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/registry.ts src/plugins/http-registry.test.ts`, `git diff --check`, and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/registry.ts src/plugins/http-registry.test.ts`.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate from the prior stack: `CRABBOX_AWS_REGION=us-east-1 CRABBOX_CAPACITY_REGIONS=us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-central-1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "corepack pnpm check:changed"`.
- Changed-file, spec, and PR-body scrub for the reported plugin name and private path patterns.

Evidence after fix:

- Latest provider-like focused proof passed: 1 file, 9 tests passed.
- Provider-like focused oxfmt check, `git diff --check`, and targeted oxlint passed for `src/plugins/registry.ts` and `src/plugins/registry.provider-like.test.ts`.
- Autoreview for the provider-like slice found a provider-instance compatibility issue; after preserving original provider objects and adding class-backed coverage, autoreview reran clean with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Recent HTTP route focused proof passed after rebase: 2 files, 48 tests passed.
- HTTP route focused oxfmt check, `git diff --check`, targeted oxlint, and autoreview passed.
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed on provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4fdce7317314`, run `run_f0ee5dfc90be`, against base `07870dff45eb` before later drift rebases.
- Current branch diff, local spec, and PR body scrub pass for the reported plugin name and private paths.

Observed result after fix:

- Synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures with unreadable names, schemas, descriptions, approval fields, provider metadata, provider-like ids, MCP catalog fields, cached runtime descriptors, Codex dynamic-tool descriptors, MCP argument preparers, Plugin SDK tool metadata, Plugin SDK Control UI descriptors, runtime lifecycle fields, agent event subscription fields, scheduler job fields, session action fields, session extension fields, trusted policy fields, reload prefix fields, node host command fields, node invoke policy fields, service fields, gateway discovery service fields, command fields, and HTTP route fields no longer crash the relevant projection/resolution/registration paths.
- Healthy siblings, such as `mockplugin_status`, `mockplugin_store`, `mockplugin-status`, `mockplugin-card`, `mockplugin-hooks`, `mockplugin-scheduler`, `mockplugin-extension`, `mockplugin-node`, `mockplugin-service`, `mockplugin-command`, `mockplugin` HTTP routes, and `mockplugin` provider-like registrations, continue through materialization, descriptor caching, provider payload conversion, MCP/Gateway listing, agent-core validation, Codex dynamic-tool bridge registration, plugin-tools MCP execution, plugin host-hook registration, HTTP route registration, and provider-like registration while malformed siblings are omitted, quarantined, or diagnosed.
- Plugin host-hook and HTTP route registration now stores sanitized metadata/descriptor/lifecycle/subscription/scheduler/action/session-extension/trusted-policy/node/service/command/route records from bounded field reads instead of spreading plugin-provided objects; provider-like registrations now fail closed on unreadable ids without replacing valid provider instances.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because `blacksmith` is not authenticated in this shell.
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed` was not rerun after the latest HTTP route and provider-like slices; the previous broad pass was on the earlier stack, and the latest slices have focused proof, formatting, targeted oxlint, and autoreview.
- GitHub CI for the newest push is still running/pending.
- No live third-party plugin was referenced or exercised; tests intentionally use synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures.

## Current review state

- PR remains draft while this fuzzing/hardening lane continues.
- Latest pushed commit: `0cd0d18975ac109881df99c0079cd586ee88e1c0` (`fix(plugins): guard provider-like registration ids`).

commands.author: MertBasar0

target.title: fix(auth): cooldown inline api key billing failures
target.body:
## Summary
- track provider-level usage/backoff state for inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials without creating synthetic auth profiles
- skip temporarily disabled inline API keys during auth resolution and availability checks
- mark inline API keys on provider auth/billing failures so repeated 402s do not immediately retry the same exhausted key

Closes #39807.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials now record provider auth/billing failures in usage state, stop immediate reuse during cooldown, and become available again after the cooldown expires without creating a synthetic auth profile.
- **Environment tested**: Local Linux workstation on PR branch `fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown` at `61ac6e93eac913fee891d3d63262518e08185c9d`, using Node/tsx against the production auth profile store, usage helpers, and model auth resolution helpers.
- **Steps run after the patch**: Ran a Node terminal probe that created a temporary agent directory, configured a dummy inline provider API key, resolved availability before failure, called `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)` with reason `billing`, checked availability/resolution during cooldown, then simulated cooldown expiry and resolved the inline key again.
- **Evidence**:

```text
[agent/embedded] auth profile failure state updated: runId=real-proof-inline-billing profile=sha256:f68577404b84 provider=demo-local reason=billing window=disabled reused=false
{
  "behavior": "inline provider apiKey billing failure disables only that inline key and blocks immediate reuse",
  "beforeFailure": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  },
  "recordedFailure": {
    "usageId": "inline-api-key:demo-local",
    "profileCreated": false,
    "disabledReason": "billing",
    "disabledUntilIsFuture": true
  },
  "duringCooldown": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": false,
    "blocked": true,
    "blockedMessage": "Inline API key for provider \"demo-local\" is temporarily disabled after a provider auth/billing failure. Retry after about 300 minutes, or switch to a different auth profile/API key."
  },
  "afterCooldownExpiry": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  }
}
```

- **Observed result**: Before the billing failure, the inline key is available and resolves from `models.json`. After `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)`, `usageStats["inline-api-key:demo-local"]` records `disabledReason: "billing"`, `disabledUntil` is in the future, no synthetic profile is created, `hasAvailableAuthForProvider(...)` returns false, and `resolveApiKeyForProvider(...)` blocks immediate reuse with the temporary-disabled error. After simulated cooldown expiry, the same inline key becomes available and resolves from `models.json` again.
- **Not tested**: A live paid provider 402 against a real provider account; this avoids intentionally causing a real billing failure while still exercising the production failure-marking and auth-resolution path with a redacted dummy inline key.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts` (2 files, 66 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Additional real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed after review**: the actual embedded-runner failure paths now call the inline provider API-key marker even when the active credential has no profile id.
- **Environment tested**: local checkout after merging upstream/main into fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown at ce378baab4.
- **Evidence after fix**: added runner-path coverage that simulates an inline models.providers.openai.apiKey billing failure with no auth profile and verifies auth-state.json records inline-api-key:openai with disabledReason: "billing" and a future disabledUntil.
- **Validation after fix**:
  - ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts
  - git diff --check
  - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot — 6 files, 156 tests passed.


## Follow-up proof after env-backed cooldown repair

- **Behavior addressed after ClawSweeper review**: models.providers.<id>.apiKey credentials backed by env markers or env SecretRefs now use the same inline provider cooldown gate as literal inline keys. Provider availability also reports env-backed inline keys unavailable while the inline cooldown window is active.
- **Availability/cache behavior**: markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...) now fires the same auth failure hook used by profile failures, so warmed provider auth state is cleared and recomputed after inline-key billing/auth failures instead of continuing to advertise stale availability.
- **Regression coverage added**:
  - configured env-marker apiKey is blocked during inline provider cooldown
  - configured env SecretRef apiKey is blocked during inline provider cooldown
  - hasAvailableAuthForProvider(...) returns false for those cooled-down env-backed inline keys
  - inline provider API-key failures trigger the auth failure hook for cache invalidation/self-heal behavior
- **Validation after fix at 5604fa8daf**:
  - pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts --reporter=dot — 4 files / 148 tests passed
  - pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot — 6 files / 176 tests passed
  - pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts
  - git diff --check
  - pnpm check:test-types


## Follow-up after assistant no-profile marker repair

- **Behavior addressed after ClawSweeper review**: assistant-stage rotate_profile handling now calls the auth failure marker even when there is no active profile id, allowing inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` billing/auth failures to record provider-level cooldown instead of immediately reusing the exhausted inline key.
- **Regression coverage added**: `handleAssistantFailover` now covers a no-profile billing rotate path and verifies `maybeMarkAuthProfileFailure({ profileId: undefined, reason: "billing", modelId })` is invoked so the runner-level inline marker can run.
- **Validation after fix at 6373aa62fe**:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-include-assistant.json node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts` — 1 file / 15 tests passed
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-include-80482.json node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts` — 3 files / 89 tests passed
  - `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 1 file / 28 tests passed
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `git diff --check`


## Follow-up after auth fallback/default-agent repair

- **Behavior addressed after ClawSweeper review**: env-backed inline API keys in cooldown no longer make the provider unavailable before checking healthy stored auth profiles for the same provider.
- **Default-agent repair**: early inline cooldown checks now use the cfg-resolved agent directory, matching the later auth-profile store resolution when callers omit `agentDir`.
- **Regression coverage added**:
  - cfg default agent dir is honored for inline cooldown checks
  - cooled env-marker inline auth still allows a healthy stored profile to keep provider auth available
  - env-marker and env SecretRef cooldown blocking still works when no alternate auth is available
- **Validation after fix at 126a12f343**:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-include-80482-p2.json node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts` — 2 files / 76 tests passed
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `git diff --check`
target.author: mertbasar0

target.title: fix(auth): cooldown inline api key billing failures
target.body:
## Summary
- track provider-level usage/backoff state for inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials without creating synthetic auth profiles
- skip temporarily disabled inline API keys during auth resolution and availability checks
- mark inline API keys on provider auth/billing failures so repeated 402s do not immediately retry the same exhausted key

Closes #39807.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials now record provider auth/billing failures in usage state, stop immediate reuse during cooldown, and become available again after the cooldown expires without creating a synthetic auth profile.
- **Environment tested**: Local Linux workstation on PR branch `fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown` at `61ac6e93eac913fee891d3d63262518e08185c9d`, using Node/tsx against the production auth profile store, usage helpers, and model auth resolution helpers.
- **Steps run after the patch**: Ran a Node terminal probe that created a temporary agent directory, configured a dummy inline provider API key, resolved availability before failure, called `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)` with reason `billing`, checked availability/resolution during cooldown, then simulated cooldown expiry and resolved the inline key again.
- **Evidence**:

```text
[agent/embedded] auth profile failure state updated: runId=real-proof-inline-billing profile=sha256:f68577404b84 provider=demo-local reason=billing window=disabled reused=false
{
  "behavior": "inline provider apiKey billing failure disables only that inline key and blocks immediate reuse",
  "beforeFailure": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  },
  "recordedFailure": {
    "usageId": "inline-api-key:demo-local",
    "profileCreated": false,
    "disabledReason": "billing",
    "disabledUntilIsFuture": true
  },
  "duringCooldown": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": false,
    "blocked": true,
    "blockedMessage": "Inline API key for provider \"demo-local\" is temporarily disabled after a provider auth/billing failure. Retry after about 300 minutes, or switch to a different auth profile/API key."
  },
  "afterCooldownExpiry": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  }
}
```

- **Observed result**: Before the billing failure, the inline key is available and resolves from `models.json`. After `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)`, `usageStats["inline-api-key:demo-local"]` records `disabledReason: "billing"`, `disabledUntil` is in the future, no synthetic profile is created, `hasAvailableAuthForProvider(...)` returns false, and `resolveApiKeyForProvider(...)` blocks immediate reuse with the temporary-disabled error. After simulated cooldown expiry, the same inline key becomes available and resolves from `models.json` again.
- **Not tested**: A live paid provider 402 against a real provider account; this avoids intentionally causing a real billing failure while still exercising the production failure-marking and auth-resolution path with a redacted dummy inline key.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts` (2 files, 66 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Additional real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed after review**: the actual embedded-runner failure paths now call the inline provider API-key marker even when the active credential has no profile id.
- **Environment tested**: local checkout after merging upstream/main into fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown at ce378baab4.
- **Evidence after fix**: added runner-path coverage that simulates an inline models.providers.openai.apiKey billing failure with no auth profile and verifies auth-state.json records inline-api-key:openai with disabledReason: "billing" and a future disabledUntil.
- **Validation after fix**:
  - ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts
  - git diff --check
  - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot — 6 files, 156 tests passed.


## Follow-up proof after env-backed cooldown repair

- **Behavior addressed after ClawSweeper review**: models.providers.<id>.apiKey credentials backed by env markers or env SecretRefs now use the same inline provider cooldown gate as literal inline keys. Provider availability also reports env-backed inline keys unavailable while the inline cooldown window is active.
- **Availability/cache behavior**: markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...) now fires the same auth failure hook used by profile failures, so warmed provider auth state is cleared and recomputed after inline-key billing/auth failures instead of continuing to advertise stale availability.
- **Regression coverage added**:
  - configured env-marker apiKey is blocked during inline provider cooldown
  - configured env SecretRef apiKey is blocked during inline provider cooldown
  - hasAvailableAuthForProvider(...) returns false for those cooled-down env-backed inline keys
  - inline provider API-key failures trigger the auth failure hook for cache invalidation/self-heal behavior
- **Validation after fix at 5604fa8daf**:
  - pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts --reporter=dot — 4 files / 148 tests passed
  - pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot — 6 files / 176 tests passed
  - pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts
  - git diff --check
  - pnpm check:test-types


## Follow-up after assistant no-profile marker repair

- **Behavior addressed after ClawSweeper review**: assistant-stage rotate_profile handling now calls the auth failure marker even when there is no active profile id, allowing inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` billing/auth failures to record provider-level cooldown instead of immediately reusing the exhausted inline key.
- **Regression coverage added**: `handleAssistantFailover` now covers a no-profile billing rotate path and verifies `maybeMarkAuthProfileFailure({ profileId: undefined, reason: "billing", modelId })` is invoked so the runner-level inline marker can run.
- **Validation after fix at 6373aa62fe**:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-include-assistant.json node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts` — 1 file / 15 tests passed
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-include-80482.json node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts` — 3 files / 89 tests passed
  - `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 1 file / 28 tests passed
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `git diff --check`


## Follow-up after auth fallback/default-agent repair

- **Behavior addressed after ClawSweeper review**: env-backed inline API keys in cooldown no longer make the provider unavailable before checking healthy stored auth profiles for the same provider.
- **Default-agent repair**: early inline cooldown checks now use the cfg-resolved agent directory, matching the later auth-profile store resolution when callers omit `agentDir`.
- **Regression coverage added**:
  - cfg default agent dir is honored for inline cooldown checks
  - cooled env-marker inline auth still allows a healthy stored profile to keep provider auth available
  - env-marker and env SecretRef cooldown blocking still works when no alternate auth is available
- **Validation after fix at 126a12f343**:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-include-80482-p2.json node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts` — 2 files / 76 tests passed
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(auth): cooldown inline api key billing failures
target.body:
## Summary
- track provider-level usage/backoff state for inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials without creating synthetic auth profiles
- skip temporarily disabled inline API keys during auth resolution and availability checks
- mark inline API keys on provider auth/billing failures so repeated 402s do not immediately retry the same exhausted key

Closes #39807.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials now record provider auth/billing failures in usage state, stop immediate reuse during cooldown, and become available again after the cooldown expires without creating a synthetic auth profile.
- **Environment tested**: Local Linux workstation on PR branch `fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown` at `61ac6e93eac913fee891d3d63262518e08185c9d`, using Node/tsx against the production auth profile store, usage helpers, and model auth resolution helpers.
- **Steps run after the patch**: Ran a Node terminal probe that created a temporary agent directory, configured a dummy inline provider API key, resolved availability before failure, called `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)` with reason `billing`, checked availability/resolution during cooldown, then simulated cooldown expiry and resolved the inline key again.
- **Evidence**:

```text
[agent/embedded] auth profile failure state updated: runId=real-proof-inline-billing profile=sha256:f68577404b84 provider=demo-local reason=billing window=disabled reused=false
{
  "behavior": "inline provider apiKey billing failure disables only that inline key and blocks immediate reuse",
  "beforeFailure": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  },
  "recordedFailure": {
    "usageId": "inline-api-key:demo-local",
    "profileCreated": false,
    "disabledReason": "billing",
    "disabledUntilIsFuture": true
  },
  "duringCooldown": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": false,
    "blocked": true,
    "blockedMessage": "Inline API key for provider \"demo-local\" is temporarily disabled after a provider auth/billing failure. Retry after about 300 minutes, or switch to a different auth profile/API key."
  },
  "afterCooldownExpiry": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  }
}
```

- **Observed result**: Before the billing failure, the inline key is available and resolves from `models.json`. After `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)`, `usageStats["inline-api-key:demo-local"]` records `disabledReason: "billing"`, `disabledUntil` is in the future, no synthetic profile is created, `hasAvailableAuthForProvider(...)` returns false, and `resolveApiKeyForProvider(...)` blocks immediate reuse with the temporary-disabled error. After simulated cooldown expiry, the same inline key becomes available and resolves from `models.json` again.
- **Not tested**: A live paid provider 402 against a real provider account; this avoids intentionally causing a real billing failure while still exercising the production failure-marking and auth-resolution path with a redacted dummy inline key.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts` (2 files, 66 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`

- **Evidence after fix**: added runner-path coverage that simulates an inline models.providers.openai.apiKey billing failure with no auth profile and verifies auth-state.json records inline-api-key:openai with disabledReason: "billing" and a future disabledUntil.
- **Validation after fix**:

  - git diff --check
  - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.run-embedded-pi-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot — 6 files, 156 tests passed.

  - inline provider API-key failures trigger the auth failure hook for cache invalidation/self-heal behavior

  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-include-80482.json node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts` — 3 files / 89 tests passed

  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `git diff --check`
